Malva, commonly known as mallow, is a plant that belongs to the Malvaceae family. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves, flowers, and roots, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Malva include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, and it is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in skincare products and as a food ingredient.
